Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.animation

Class FlixelSpritemapJsonLoader

java.lang.Object
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.animation.FlixelSpritemapJsonLoader
public final class FlixelSpritemapJsonLoader extends Object
Helper class for spritemap-style JSON assets. Handles two separate shapes:
  1. Simple animation index - a top-level "animations": { name: { "frames": [0, 1, 2] } } block (with optional framerate). Frames may be declared as a JSON array of [x, y, w, h, ...] rows, as an object map of frame name to {"frame": {"x", "y", "w", "h"}}, or as an Adobe "TexturePacker" ATLAS.SPRITES block.
  2. Adobe Animate texture atlas - the usual Animate export shape: top-level "AN" in Animation.json plus ATLAS.SPRITES in the spritemap file. Nested symbol rigs and flat document timelines are routed to FlixelAnimateRigLoader, which requires the owning sprite to be a FlixelAnimateSprite.

Game code does not usually call this helper directly. A FlixelAnimateSprite accepts an Animate atlas triple through FlixelAnimateSprite.addSpritesheetAndAnimation(java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String), and a plain FlixelSprite accepts a simple-format pair through FlixelAnimationController.loadSpritemapFromJson(java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String).

    Parses a spritemap JSON's ATLAS.SPRITES block (the Adobe Animate "BTA" shape) into a list of FlixelFrame instances, and fills nameToIndexOut with a lookup from sprite name to atlas index.

  • Method Details

    • resolveAssetPath

      @NotNull public static @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.files.FileHandle resolveAssetPath(@NotNull @NotNull String path)
      Resolves an asset path the same way the rest of FlixelGDX does: first Gdx.files.internal, then Gdx.files.classpath. The first hit that exists and is not a directory is returned.
      Parameters:
      path - The path to resolve. Must not be null or empty.
      Returns:
      The resolved FileHandle. Never null.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If the path resolves to nothing readable.

    • readUtf8Text

      @NotNull public static @NotNull String readUtf8Text(@NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.files.FileHandle f)
      Reads a text file as UTF-8 and strips a leading byte-order mark if present. Use for XML and JSON so the path does not fall back to the platform default charset.
      Parameters:
      f - The file handle to read from. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      The text content; never null (an empty file yields the empty string).

    • parseAtlasSprites

      @NotNull public static @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Array<FlixelFrame> parseAtlasSprites(@NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.utils.JsonValue spritemapRoot, @NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Texture texture, @NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.utils.ObjectMap<String,Integer> nameToIndexOut)
      Parses a spritemap JSON's ATLAS.SPRITES block (the Adobe Animate "BTA" shape) into a list of FlixelFrame instances, and fills nameToIndexOut with a lookup from sprite name to atlas index.

      This method is shared between the simple and Animate loaders so that only one place knows how to unpack Adobe's name/x/y/w/h fields.

      Parameters:
      spritemapRoot - The root JSON value of the spritemap file. Must not be null.
      texture - The backing texture to wrap each region around. Must not be null.
      nameToIndexOut - An output map. Cleared on entry and filled with sprite name to atlas index for each entry that has a non-empty name. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      The list of built FlixelFrame regions, in declaration order.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If ATLAS.SPRITES is missing, not an array, or contains zero entries.

    • load

      public static void load(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelAnimationController controller, @NotNull @NotNull String textureKey, @NotNull @NotNull String spritemapJsonPath, @NotNull @NotNull String animationJsonPath)
      Top-level entry point used by FlixelAnimationController.loadSpritemapFromJson(java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String). Reads both files, figures out which flavor the animation JSON is, and dispatches to the appropriate handler.
      Parameters:
      controller - The animation controller whose owning sprite should receive the loaded clips. Must not be null.
      textureKey - The asset key of the backing PNG. Must not be null.
      spritemapJsonPath - The path to the spritemap JSON. Must not be null.
      animationJsonPath - The path to the animation JSON. Must not be null.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If either file is malformed, or if an Adobe Animate JSON is being loaded onto a sprite that is not a FlixelAnimateSprite.
